title: Student reviews for Professor Alex Ryba
text:
Source: Rate My Professor
Document Title: Student reviews for Professor Alex Ryba
School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/44623

Review 1
Course: CSCI212
Sentiment: Mixed
Review Text:
Decent professor. Maybe attended 8 lectures, I deserved what I got. His exams contain lots of syntax. Quite hard to work with 1 on 1. Lectures were heavy and honestly a drag, but informative. Practice coding outside of the class and you'll get a great grade. Don't skip lab, it makes a pretty decent part of your grade. Not hard to pass at all.

title: Student reviews for Professor Alex Ryba
text:
Source: Rate My Professor
Document Title: Student reviews for Professor Alex Ryba
School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/44623

Review 2
Course: CSCI212
Sentiment: Mixed
Review Text:
Rated a 4 bc lectures are clear, lots of examples--BUT the info is a lot! A recurring pattern of putting in topics in the exams that are kind of glossed over/not prioritized in lab. Curves generously. Doesn't give much info abt the exams' contents, but there are practice problems.

title: Student reviews for Professor Alex Ryba
text:
Source: Rate My Professor
Document Title: Student reviews for Professor Alex Ryba
School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/44623

Review 3
Course: CSCI331
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
Try not to take the early morning sections if you aren't a morning person! Nonetheless, this is the ideal course to learn and apply the knowledge of data structures! You'll need to regularly dedicate much practice for each concept as you'll never know when a quiz is looming around! The curveballs in exams truly test your problem solving and wit!

title: Student reviews for Professor Alex Ryba
text:
Source: Rate My Professor
Document Title: Student reviews for Professor Alex Ryba
School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/44623

Review 4
Course: CSCI111
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
Professor Ryba is one of the best in Queens College, and the opportunity to start your knowledge here was an absolute pleasure! His lectures serve to introduce the overall concepts within Computer Science with C++ under the scope of iostream and the C standard lib. Theory alone won't get you a grade but problem solving and practicing syntax will.

title: Student reviews for Professor Alex Ryba
text:
Source: Rate My Professor
Document Title: Student reviews for Professor Alex Ryba
School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/44623

Review 5
Course: CSCI320
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
Absolutely the best, super nice and down to earth guy that is very knowledgeable about what he's teaching. He really seems to care about the students. I got a pretty mediocre score on the midterm, missed the second midterm, and did very good on the final and he gave me an A. He definitely notices your improvement and rewards you for it.

title: Student reviews for Professor Alex Ryba
text:
Source: Rate My Professor
Document Title: Student reviews for Professor Alex Ryba
School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/44623

Review 6
Course: CSCI111
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
Ryba's CS111 is no easy feat, but with diligent practice using his review packets and tools like unstuckstudy, you can master the basics and earn a solid grade. Lectures are informative, and the curve helps offset the challenge.

title: Student reviews for Professor Alex Ryba
text:
Source: Rate My Professor
Document Title: Student reviews for Professor Alex Ryba
School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/44623

Review 7
Course: CSCI111
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
First time taking C++ after being scared about it but he explains everything really well. He only has 3 tests, but will replace your score if you fail on the first 2 exams but pass the final. Tests are a LOT harder than what he goes over in class. Curve carries your grade. I got a 19/30 (63.33%) on the final, yet I passed the class with an A.

title: Student reviews for Professor Alex Ryba
text:
Source: Rate My Professor
Document Title: Student reviews for Professor Alex Ryba
School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/44623

Review 8
Course: CSCI111
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
Dr.Ryba is a fantastic lecturer, who made use of many "real world" examples throughout his classes to solidify concepts. Attendance is not mandatory, he records the lectures and posts them all online.Ultimately, to do well in this course, do many problems which he provides from past exams. practice problems are your key to the A.

title: Student reviews for Professor Alex Ryba
text:
Source: Rate My Professor
Document Title: Student reviews for Professor Alex Ryba
School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/44623

Review 9
Course: CSCI111
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
Really good teacher. This class can be taken online or in person. Attendance is not mandatory and he posts recording of the class. Easy to understand as a first time coder in C++. i recommend to take him. Fun and chill guy. The textbook is around 30 but there is free pdf online. 10/10 professor!

title: Student reviews for Professor Alex Ryba
text:
Source: Rate My Professor
Document Title: Student reviews for Professor Alex Ryba
School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/44623

Review 10
Course: CSCI111
Sentiment: Negative
Review Text:
He isn't as good as previous reviews state. He is too lazy to go after cheaters which according to him made up over half of the class. That made the curve smaller cause cheaters would score higher. Lectures are the most basic uses of the c++ language while tests are much more complex. Offers no help when asked as he says your lab professor should.

title: Student reviews for Professor Anne Smith-Thompson
text:

School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/352320

Review 1
Course: CSCI316
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
I failed both Kong and Tao. They were both awful professors—very mean and insincere. Then I took Anne. I attended every class and put effort into all my assignments and quizzes. Everything was based on what we learned. She's a bit disorganized and grades things late, but she's one of the sweetest women ever.

title: Student reviews for Professor Anne Smith-Thompson

Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/352320

Review 2
Course: CSCI340
Sentiment: Negative
Review Text:
She cant teach at all. The part where she goes on tangents is true and she gets mad if you try to reach out to her after hours. You arerelying on zybooks and sheer luck to know anything on her exams as she doesn't do proper review for her classes(except maybe 316). She doesn't even post class notes or recordings on time.

title: Student reviews for Professor Anne Smith-Thompson

URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/352320

Review 3
Course: CSCI343
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
Prof Thompson is one of the best CS professors in QC. She is caring and always willing to help once you show that your putting in the work. Go to class, take notes, study and you will be fine. She gives a quiz every week which helps keep you on top of things. She give extra credit points for stuff. She's not out here just trying to fail people.

Review 4
Course: CSCI313
Sentiment: Negative
Review Text:
She is insufferable, expects students to know the material beforehand, cancels classes frequently, and is super slow to grade. Her teaching is poor, yet her exams are overly difficult. Take her only if you already know the subject.

Course: CSCI313
Sentiment: Negative
Review Text:
She is super unorganized and will make your life HELL. She barely taught the basics in the class and midterm was so hard. It was nothing like stuff we learned in the class. She's so unfair. Makes classes asynchronous every week says recordings will be posted and then never posts the recordings for it. UNFAIIIRRR.

title: Student reviews for Professor Anne Smith-Thompson

Review 6
Course: CSCI343
Sentiment: Mixed
Review Text:
Average professor: many quizzes, difficult tests, and her personality is somewhat abrasive. She cares and wants you to pass though, She holds a lot of review lectures outside of class. She does require you to pay for Zybooks for homework though. Just do all the work assigned in a timely manner and take the class very seriously.

title: Student reviews for Professor Anne Smith-Thompson

URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/352320

Review 7
Course: CSCI340
Sentiment: Negative
Review Text:
Anne does go off topic but not as frequently as many say. Class is very chaotic and she'll frequently cancel class at the last minute. Takes FOREVER to post grades for anything. Doesn't respond to emails and generally seems very apathetic towards the class. Perfect representation of the CS department for a joke of an institution like QC.

title: Student reviews for Professor Anne Smith-Thompson

URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/352320

Review 8
Course: CSCI340
Sentiment: Mixed
Review Text:
While she does go on tangents a lot, it usually relates to the material. You will likely have to learn from the textbook though. Her exams are definitely not too hard if you put in the work. She can be kind of harsh grading the programming assignments though. She is generally nice but can be a bit mean if the class didnt do well on the exam.

title: Student reviews for Professor Anne Smith-Thompson

Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/352320

Review 9
Course: CSCI343
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
Make sure you listen a LOT to what professor says in class. She will test you heavily on what she goes over more frequently in class. Then, read the textbook chapter of the specific topic you went over in class. Go to office hours and ask specific questions on what to go over before a test, and go over topics you struggle with in office hours.

title: Student reviews for Professor Anne Smith-Thompson

Review 10
Course: CSCI343
Sentiment: Mixed
Review Text:
Professor Anne was ok, I didnt really learn much and she never showed up to class however she is very nice and even though I failed half the quizzes hard, she told us what would be on the final and the final itself was a copy paste of the quizzes with a curve and I got a 95 on the final somehow. I ended up with a B, Easy to pass but wont learn.

title: Student reviews for Professor Bojana Obrenic
text:
Source: Rate My Professor

School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/249702

Review 1
Course: CSCI320
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
She has a good curve for tests. As long as you practice questions from her problem book and learn on ur own or by going to her lectures, you will be good. Her problem book serves as an example of how her exams look like. She even puts extra questions on exam for a better chance at passing. Waaaay better than Boklan, for passing the course.

title: Student reviews for Professor Bojana Obrenic
text:
Source: Rate My Professor
Document Title: Student reviews for Professor Bojana Obrenic
School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/249702

Review 2
Course: CSCI320
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
She is nice. She is caring and is considerate of students. Go to class, participate, and you will learn a lot. Just relying upon previous professors' reviews is not enough. Review materials can be enough to pass, but for A, you have to work for it. Best of 2 exams. She gives a lot of extra credits, seriously, a lot. Go to class, and you will learn.

title: Student reviews for Professor Bojana Obrenic
text:
Source: Rate My Professor
Document Title: Student reviews for Professor Bojana Obrenic
School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/249702

Review 3
Course: CSCI320
Sentiment: Mixed
Review Text:
If you do well on midterm 1 and midterm 2 , you dont need to take the final. I wind up taking final which you need at least a 60 to be consider passing her class. I took her during the summer. Exam 1 is regular expression, godel numbers, dfa, ndfa, telescoping and cfg Exam 2 is Pump Lemma, Turing Machine and exam 1 stuff Final is exam 1&2.

title: Student reviews for Professor Bojana Obrenic
text:

School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/249702

Review 4
Course: CSCI320
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
320 can be difficult but with her curve (taking the higher 2 out of the 3 exams & only 40 pts is a C) she is VERY passable. So many resources out there as others have said, legit do the problem book and ur good! Is very tough-love, always says “she'll beat you up if your answer is garbage”, but means well & is entertaining! Truly the canon choice.

title: Student reviews for Professor Bojana Obrenic
text:

School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/249702

Review 5
Course: CSCI320
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
Professor Obrenic is undoubtedly the funniest professor in QC history. If you take the time to go to her lectures and get beaten up (iykyk), you'll have a great time and it will make the exams easier. The exams are difficult by time constraint, but they are more than studyable. The bar for a C is also quite low (80 point sum between 2 exams).

title: Student reviews for Professor Bojana Obrenic
text:

School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/249702

Review 6
Course: CSCI331
Sentiment: Mixed
Review Text:
Similar style lectures to her 320. More problem solving tests. Same super generous grading scale. If you liked her 320, you'll like her 331. If you didn't like her 320, don't take 331. A lot of theory, but also heavy in SQL, datalog, relational algebra. My only warning is that if you relied on Clint for 320, you don't have that clutch anymore.

Review 7
Course: CSCI320
Sentiment: Negative
Review Text:
I graduated a while ago. Looking back, this is the worst prof I've ever had in my entire life. Every where I went to complain about her, they knew her by name, even before I say her name, and they would just tell me that lots of students complain about her regularly. Yet nobody holds her accountable. She has to be investigated! Easy pass though.

Review 8
Course: CSCI320
Sentiment: Mixed
Review Text:
2nd best option for 320 besides Ryba. Graded off 3 exams, have to average a 40 for a C. Exam problems are very unintuitive and require Clint videos for guidance on how to approach them. Go to one of the CS club discords and join the 320 server to watch his vids. Without him exams are borderline impossible. Her lectures are entirely useless as well.

title: Student reviews for Professor Bojana Obrenic

URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/249702

Review 9
Course: CSCI320
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
Grading criteria is generous. I did well on my first tests, skipped the final and still got A+. Idk why people decide to skip class and only watch Clint's videos; do both and everything will be clear She gives no HW and has a problem book of every exam question she's ever given. EC opportunities. Most fascinating lectures at QC. Can be abrasive.

only positive thing was the simple grading criteria- 3 tests with the lowest dropped. The course is EASY and USELESS. Terrible attitude when someone asks a question (she refuses to answer and only asks if theres a mistake with what she wrote), she tries to be funny but is only successful 5% of the time. And the worst is her handwriting, unreadable

title: Student reviews for Professor Jackson Yeh
text:
Source: Rate My Professor
Document Title: Student reviews for Professor Jackson Yeh
School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/1082596

Review 1
Course: CSCI240
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
2 quizzes you can do multiple times to get 100%, a midterm and final, and a Logisim project, which he goes over in class. Very straightforward teaching; good examples to practice during class. The content can be difficult even if it's online; you definitely should take notes (exams are open-book). Wants students to do well and participate.

title: Student reviews for Professor Jackson Yeh
text:
Source: Rate My Professor
Document Title: Student reviews for Professor Jackson Yeh
School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/1082596

Review 2
Course: CSCI240
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
Took him for 4 week summer which is VERY accelerated. Open notes midterm and final. Circuit project we did 80% together so that was free 100. Advice: BRUSH UP ON UR BINARY. It's used the whole class and he speeds through it. His lectures are too fast to write everything down in a notebook (I tried) so I suggest just taking notes on the pdf itself.

title: Student reviews for Professor Jackson Yeh
text:
Source: Rate My Professor

School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/1082596

Review 3
Course: CSCI240
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
The class is online. Dr. Yeh records all lectures and posts them so you can rewatch them if you miss class. Try your best not to fall back as concepts tend to build upon one another. Overall, class not too difficult as the exams/quizzes are online. Hardest part is the project in logisim. Otherwise, he is the best choice for 240 at QC, hands down.

title: Student reviews for Professor Jackson Yeh
text:

Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/1082596

Review 4
Course: CSCI240
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
Professor Yeh, the goat. Just take his class. He has two exams (midterm and final), both open-book and online, and the circuit project is manageable. His lectures are recorded. Reviews say he is rude, but he was never rude, people are just soft. A chill professor and one of the best in the QC CS dept. I know people struggling with other professors.

title: Student reviews for Professor Jackson Yeh

Review 5
Course: CSCI240
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
he's really good for the class, except with MIPS because its harder. does not respond to emails, but his lectures are clear. the project is very easy, and he explains everything so well!! The midterm was a joke (just on the first chapter), the final is a bit more difficult, but everything is online and he lets you use your notes

title: Student reviews for Professor Jackson Yeh

Review 6
Course: CSCI240
Sentiment: Negative
Review Text:
Overrated instructor who does his job in ways that are most convenient to himself. Students' learning comes secondary. Some students happen to benefit from it and most are hurt. No wonder everyone had such a rude awakening looking at their exam results. Nonetheless, he's still better than most of the other CS instructors.

title: Student reviews for Professor Jackson Yeh

URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/1082596

Review 7
Course: CSCI240
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
There is a curve on the overall grade. Textbook is not needed to do well. Recordings are enough to help understand the material. Very lenient on project deadlines. Clarifies unclear material & answers question very well during class. At times he takes a while to respond to emails or does not at all respond to them. He can be sarcastic & humorous

Course: CSCI240
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
GREAT PROFFESOR!! Just don't do any funny bussiness during the exams like no cams during exams or being buck naked. 2 exams, 2 quizzes and a project. For the summer class I took, he waived the project because it was just a 4 week course. Easy A if you study the class slides.

title: Student reviews for Professor Jackson Yeh

URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/1082596

Review 9
Course: CSCI240
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
Can't say anything more about this professor other than the fact that he is the GOAT. He is the best option for 240, especially during the summer, and he teaches in a way that is very easy to understand. He also records lectures and the exams were pretty fair. One of the only good CS professors in the department, could never ask for more.

title: Student reviews for Professor Jackson Yeh

URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/1082596

Review 10
Course: CSCI240
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
Is very clear during lectures and has a clear grading criteria. Two exams, one project, and quizzes (free grade). He records lectures so participation doesn't matter however you should still make sure you're not behind or else you'll struggle. Exams were moderate. Overall, a decent professor who can teach properly!

title: Student reviews for Professor Jerry Waxman
text:
Source: Rate My Professor
Document Title: Student reviews for Professor Jerry Waxman
School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/287312

Review 1
Course: CSCI211
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
Went to ~60% of lectures, attended lab only for quizzes, went to the final 80 minutes late. Got an A. Amazing professor, fantastic lecturing ability. Content may be difficult, but most enlightening. Do projects by yourself, use ai only to teach you concepts. Attending, participating, and meditating on the concepts will get you mastery of C++.

title: Student reviews for Professor Jerry Waxman

Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/287312

Review 2
Course: CSCI381
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
You do not take this professor for a grade, you seek his teachings through this course to LEARN. I've long graduated, yet many of his core teachings have not left me. You are part of the best of the best Prof! Thank you for making such a cognitively difficult and overloaded course so enjoyable and memorable!

title: Student reviews for Professor Jerry Waxman
text:
Source: Rate My Professor
Document Title: Student reviews for Professor Jerry Waxman
School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/287312

Review 3
Course: CSCI211
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
He is a good prof. He will answer all your questions if you ask him. Some students get low grades because they don't attend his class or don't review their notebooks. You should listen carefully and take notes, it will be helpful for exams. He curves based on the class average. I recommend taking his class.

title: Student reviews for Professor Jerry Waxman
text:
Source: Rate My Professor
Document Title: Student reviews for Professor Jerry Waxman
School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/287312

Review 4
Course: CSCI211
Sentiment: Positive
Review Text:
I never went to labs so I got a B. His exams were trivial, i.e. you should have no problems if you did the work. Waxman himself is actually reasonable and nice, it's that he's seen so much BS from students over the years. Just do the work and it's an easy A+. If not, lock in because CS gets much harder than this.

title: Student reviews for Professor Jerry Waxman
text:
Source: Rate My Professor
Document Title: Student reviews for Professor Jerry Waxman
School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/287312

Review 5
Course: CSCI211
Sentiment: Negative
Review Text:
I took his class in the fall, and the experience was very difficult. His handwriting was hard to read, and no technology was allowed. If a phone was used, he would stop the lecture to address it. I found it easier to learn the material on my own. I would not recommend this class if GPA is a concern.

title: Student reviews for Professor Jerry Waxman
text:
Source: Rate My Professor
Document Title: Student reviews for Professor Jerry Waxman
School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/287312

Review 6
Course: CSCI211
Sentiment: Mixed
Review Text:
Waxman is a charismatic guy but his explanations can be overly complex sometimes. He gives a vague idea about what would be on his tests but they're moderately difficult since all you have to really do is remember the projects. His curves ease the stress and his tests are mainly fill in the blanks or “enter the output” questions.

title: Student reviews for Professor Jerry Waxman
text:
Source: Rate My Professor
Document Title: Student reviews for Professor Jerry Waxman
School: Queens College
Department: Computer Science
URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/287312

Review 7
Course: CSCI211
Sentiment: Negative
Review Text:
Worst experience in QC, you won't learn anything besides memorizing projects. Waxman loves to gatekeep what is on the test and will make his exams as hard as possible to the point where his curves can't even save you. Learned more from 211 review sessions that weren't even from him than all his lectures combined. Please avoid him and his textbooks.

Review 8

Sentiment: Mixed
Review Text:
Waxman gets a bad rep, but honestly 211 is more of a weed out class. At the end of each test, we were given a graph that shows how well you did and more than 60% of the class did so bad, which ends up helping you cause he gives a massive curve. Currently taking 300 level courses, and there are people who still don't know how to create Classes.

She was good for CS111 but not for higher level ig. can't really teach the materials, don't give clear concept. Though she's lenient with test as the questions are mostly similar to the one, she does in class. But if you wanna learn something, there're better options!

While this class was maybe not her specialty, I felt like I got a good grasp of general concepts and expanded my world a little bit. While her course was a touch easier than the other ones, I didn't feel too lost looking at other Prof's 340 exams. Plenty of opportunities for extra credit to help you pass. Exams as always, were extremely fair.

She is a very good explainer. She'll try her best to make you understand the topic you're unable to. She records her class and shares it online with classsnotes included. If you review and pratice enough you should be able to get a good grade.
